title: Proclamation No. 1756, s. 1978
text:
MALACANANG Manila

BY THE PRESIDENT & PRIME MINISTER OF THE PHILIPPINES

PROCLAMATION NO. 1756

DECLARING SATURDAY, SEPTEMBER 9, 1978, AS A SPECIAL PUBLIC HOLIDAY IN COMMEMORATION OF THE 100TH BIRTH ANNIVERSARY OF DON SERGIO OSMEÑA, SR.

WHEREAS, Don Sergio Osmeña, Sr. former President of the Philippine Commonwealth, was one of the principal architects or our Republic and a great Filipino who deserves to be honored, remembered and emulated by the nation;

WHEREAS, throughout his public career, he had always espoused the caused of national unity, thus earning for himself the distinct honor of being called the Apostle of Unity; and

WHEREAS, his life and works find relevance to the aims, goals and objectives of the New Society;

NOW, THEREFORE, I, FERDINAND E. MARCOS, President and Prime Minister of the Philippines, by virtue of the powers vested in me by law, do hereby declare Saturday, September 9, 1978, as a special public holiday in commemoration of the 100th birth anniversary of Don Sergio Osmeña, Sr.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, I have hereunto set my hand and caused the seal of the Republic of the Philippines to be affixed.

Done in the City of Manila, this 7th day of July in the year of Our Lord, nineteen hundred and seventy-eight.

(SGD.) FERDINAND E. MARCOS President & Prime Minister of the Philippines

By the President & Prime Minister:

(SGD.) JUAN C. TUVERA Presidential Assistant

Source: CDAsia
